Based on the AI model's prediction,
The fact that the checking account balance is negative, because of negative liquidity constraints, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that increases risk.[1]
The fact that there is no checking account, because of the absence of account status, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that increases risk.[2]
The fact that the loan duration is extended, because of longer repayment horizons, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that increases risk.[3]
Overall, this applicant is likely to have a high risk of default.
